skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI video prompts and run inference.sh belt CLI workflows for text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar animation, video editing, upscaling, and audio effects.
skill.summary: Create AI video workflows with inference.sh belt and major video generation models.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command and URL patterns, but review shows they are documented belt CLI examples for an AI video workflow, not hidden execution or exfiltration. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 75 are false positives caused by ordinary descriptive text. Residual risk remains medium because the skill instructs users to authenticate to and send prompts, image URLs, audio URLs, and video URLs to external inference.sh services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution for Remote Video Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill permits and documents belt CLI commands that authenticate with and run remote AI video applications. This is an expected feature, but it executes external commands and can transmit user prompts or media references to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools entry explicitly permits belt commands, and examples invoke belt app run. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but the external execution and remote processing risk is clear.

medium_findings.title: User Media URLs Sent to External Services
medium_findings.description: Several examples pass image, audio, and video URLs into remote model runs. Users may expose private media or sensitive source URLs if they use non-public assets without reviewing provider handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly show URL fields supplied to model invocations. The risk depends on user inputs and provider policy, so it is medium rather than high.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, an image asset, install instructions, and placeholder user media URLs. They support documentation and examples rather than covert network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible markdown links or placeholder media inputs. No evidence found of obfuscation, tracking endpoints, or credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The high static findings at lines 3 and 75 do not reference cryptographic functions or algorithms. They appear to match ordinary prose in the description and table header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation or configuration on these lines. The evidence is limited to descriptive text and a markdown table header.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Command Invocation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented pattern runs belt app run with JSON input. Users should review command parameters before execution because prompts and media URLs are sent to external model providers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and repeated across the skill. It is not malicious by itself, but it is a meaningful execution and data-transfer pattern.

content.user_title: Create AI Videos with belt CLI
content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable ways to create videos from prompts, images, audio, and source clips. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through inference.sh belt workflows for major AI video models.
actual_capabilities: Selects documented inference.sh video model IDs for text-to-video and image-to-video tasks.
Drafts belt CLI commands for Veo, Seedance, HappyHorse, Wan, Grok, and related video tools.
Supports avatar, lipsync, foley, upscaling, video editing, and media merge examples.
Maps user goals to prompt fields such as duration, resolution, audio generation, and source media URLs.
Points users to related video, image, speech, and social posting skills.
limitations: Requires a configured inference.sh belt CLI account before commands can run.
Does not generate videos locally inside Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
Model availability, pricing, quality, and parameters can change on the provider side.
User-supplied media URLs may be processed by external services.

use_cases.title: Prototype Short Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Create model-specific commands for short product clips, social posts, and campaign visuals.

use_cases.title: Animate Existing Images
use_cases.description: Turn portraits, product renders, or concept art into image-to-video prompts with motion direction.

use_cases.title: Build Video Automation Workflows
use_cases.description: Combine generation, editing, upscaling, foley, and merging steps into repeatable CLI workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a belt command for a 5 second text-to-video clip about [scene]. Use a fast model and include a clear prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a belt command that animates this image URL: [image URL]. The motion should be [motion direction], and the mood should be [style].

prompt_templates.title: Create an Avatar Clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a belt workflow for a talking avatar using [portrait URL] and [audio URL]. Recommend the best listed model and explain the inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Step Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a belt CLI pipeline that generates [video concept], adds sound effects, upscales the result, and merges it with [other clip URL].

output_examples.input: Create a short video of a product rotating on a clean studio background.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a fast text-to-video model, writes a concise prompt, and prepares a belt command with duration and quality settings.

output_examples.input: Animate this portrait so the subject speaks from my narration file.
output_examples.output: The skill selects an avatar or lipsync model, identifies the required image and audio URL inputs, and prepares the command sequence.

output_examples.input: Improve a silent generated clip for a social post.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes foley, upscaling, and merge steps so the clip can gain sound, better resolution, and a final transition.

best_practices: Use public or intentionally shared media URLs when sending assets to remote video models.
Start with fast models for drafts, then switch to higher quality models for final renders.
Keep prompts specific about subject, camera motion, duration, resolution, and audio needs.
anti_patterns: Do not send private portraits, audio, or unreleased product media without approval.
Do not assume every model supports the same input fields or maximum duration.
Do not run generated CLI commands before checking provider, cost, and account context.

faq.question: Does this skill create videos directly inside the chat?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare and run inference.sh belt CLI commands that call external video models.

faq.question: Which AI assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes. The quick start shows belt login before running model commands.

faq.question: Can it use an existing image or video?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes image-to-video, reference-to-video, video editing, foley, upscaling, and media merge examples.

faq.question: What should I check before running commands?
faq.answer: Review the model ID, input URLs, duration, resolution, expected cost, and provider data handling.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish in a marketplace?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk label. The commands are visible and functional, but they use external services.
